sanskrit:
मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः।
परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्॥
transliteration:
mūḍha-grāheṇātmano yat pīḍayā kriyate tapaḥ
parasyotsādanārthaṃ vā tat tāmasam udāhṛtam
literal_meaning: That austerity which is undertaken with foolish obstinacy, by torturing oneself, or with the aim of destroying others, is declared to be in the mode of darkness.
contextual_meaning: Self-punishment driven by grandiosity, revenge, or magical thinking is not spiritual growth but a shadowy power-trip masquerading as discipline.
psychological_state: Narcissistic defense against core shame: the person escalates self-harm or harm-to-others to prove omnipotence and avoid feeling small.
trigger_scenario: An executive who starves himself, sleeps only 3 h, and publicly brags about it to intimidate rivals and mask a fear of being average.
anchor_text: True discipline never harms you or others.
